m_Item1.QuestionText: Genericity feature is supported in C++ using
m_Item1.Explanation: A C++ template is a powerful feature added to C++. It allows you to define the generic classes and generic functions and thus provides support for generic programming. Generic programming is a technique where generic types are used as parameters in algorithms so that they can work for a variety of data types.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Pick the correct statement.

Options.OptionText: STL uses the concept of templates classes and functions to achive generalized implementation.

m_Item1.Explanation: STL is a generalized library and components of STL are parameterized. STL uses the concept of templates classes and function to achieve generalized implementation.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How many components STL has?
m_Item1.Explanation: STL has four components namely Algorithms, Containers, Functors and Iterators.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of a function template in C++?

Options.OptionText: To define a family of functions
To store multiple data types in a single container

m_Item1.Explanation: Function templates allow the definition of functions that can operate with any data type, which is specified at compile time, thereby defining a family of functions.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which keyword is used to define a function template?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'template' keyword is used to define a template for a function or class.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What must be included in the template definition?
m_Item1.Explanation: A template definition must include the 'template' keyword followed by template parameters in angle brackets.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following correctly defines a function template that takes a single argument?
m_Item1.Explanation: The correct syntax is using 'template <typename T>' or 'template <class T>' before the function return type and definition.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is true about function templates?

Options.OptionText: They can have both typed and template parameters.
They must return an integer type.

m_Item1.Explanation: Function templates can include both template and specific type parameters, allowing for versatile function signatures.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How does C++ determine which version of a template function to instantiate?

Options.OptionText: By guessing the intended data type
By analyzing the arguments passed to the function

m_Item1.Explanation: The C++ compiler determines the specific version of the template function to instantiate based on the arguments provided in the call.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What happens if you call a function template with arguments of different types without specializing it?

Options.OptionText: The program will compile but may crash
The call will result in an ambiguity error
The compiler will automatically generate a new function from the template
The program will produce a link-time error

m_Item1.Explanation: If the arguments are correctly matching for a template type, the compiler instantiates a new version of the function with the given types.

m_Item1.QuestionText: When are template arguments inferred by the compiler?

Options.OptionText: Only when all arguments can be equally interpreted
When function arguments match the defined template parameter types
When the template is part of the standard library

m_Item1.Explanation: Template arguments are inferred when the function arguments match the template parameter types, allowing the compiler to deduce the types.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is a non-type template parameter?

Options.OptionText: A template parameter without a default value
A parameter not related to class templates
A template parameter that is not a data type
A parameter that can only be used in constexpr contexts

m_Item1.Explanation: Non-type template parameters are parameters for templates that involve constant values like integer, character, etc., instead of types.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Can template parameters have default values?

Options.OptionText: Yes, but only for class templates
Yes, for both function and class templates
No, template parameters cannot have default values

m_Item1.Explanation: Both function and class templates can have template parameters with default values that the compiler uses if no explicit argument is provided.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a correct example of a function template with multiple parameters?

Options.OptionText: template <typename T1, typename T2> void func(T1 arg1, T1 arg2);
template <class T1, class T2> void func(T1 arg1, T2 arg2);
template <type T1, T2> func(T1 arg1, T2 arg2);
template void func<class T1, T2>(T1 arg1, T2 arg2);

m_Item1.Explanation: The correct syntax involves using multiple template parameters defined with 'typename' or 'class' before the function definition.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is template specialization?

Options.OptionText: A process to create a unique template implementation for specific data types
A strategy for optimizing template function instantiation
A method to convert a class template into a function template

m_Item1.Explanation: Template specialization allows defining a specific implementation of a template for a particular type or set of types.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following describes function template instantiation?

Options.OptionText: The automatic generation of a new function from a template when it is called
The manual specification of function parameters within a template
The process of assigning types to a templated class
A pointer assignment operation with template deduction

m_Item1.Explanation: Function template instantiation occurs when the compiler automatically creates a function based on the template and the provided arguments.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do templates facilitate code reusability in C++?

Options.OptionText: By reducing duplication through parametric polymorphism
By enforcing strict data type checking

m_Item1.Explanation: Templates promote code reusability through parametric polymorphism by allowing functions and classes to operate with generic types.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which is NOT a valid reason to use templates in C++?

Options.OptionText: To write one function template instead of multiple overloaded functions for different types
To achieve faster execution of functions through constexpr evaluation
To define a single algorithm that works for any data type

m_Item1.Explanation: Templates are not specifically aimed at performance optimization, especially via constexpr evaluation, but rather at code reusability and type generalization.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the 'typename' keyword in a template parameter list indicate?

Options.OptionText: The parameter can be any data type
The parameter must be an integral type
The parameter can only be a floating-point type

m_Item1.Explanation: 'typename' indicates that the template parameter can represent any data type.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Why might you use template specialization?

Options.OptionText: To provide a specific implementation for certain data types
To enhance the performance of all template instantiations
To automatically infer types during compilation
To restrict template instantiation to specific types

m_Item1.Explanation: Template specialization allows a tailored implementation for specific data types, enhancing flexibility and control.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary difference between 'typename' and 'class' in template parameter declaration?

Options.OptionText: 'typename' is used only in function templates, while 'class' is used in class templates
The two keywords are interchangeable in the context of template parameter declarations
'typename' indicates a higher level of abstraction than 'class'
'class' can only be used for type parameters, not non-type parameters

m_Item1.Explanation: In template parameter declaration, 'typename' and 'class' are interchangeable and mean the same thing.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can function templates be overloaded?

Options.OptionText: By specifying different numbers of template parameters
By defining functions with different numbers or types of parameters
By providing a template specialization for each overload

m_Item1.Explanation: Function templates can be overloaded by defining versions with different numbers or types of parameters, similar to regular function overloading.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What will happen if a template function is called with a type that does not support an operation used inside the template?

Options.OptionText: The function will compile successfully but produce incorrect results
The program will generate a run-time error
The compile-time error will indicate that the operation is not supported for the type
The template engine will attempt to convert the type

m_Item1.Explanation: If a template function is called with a type that doesn't support an operation, a compile-time error will occur, indicating that the operation isn't supported.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is a class template in C++ used for?

Options.OptionText: To store different data types in a single container
To define a blueprint for classes
To allow type parameters in classes

m_Item1.Explanation: Class templates in C++ allow classes to have type parameters.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which keyword is used to define a class template in C++?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'template' keyword is used to define a class template in C++.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What syntax is used to declare a class template in C++?
m_Item1.Explanation: The correct syntax for declaring a class template is 'template<class T>'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which is a correct instantiation of a class template?
m_Item1.Explanation: To instantiate a class template, you need to specify the type, e.g., 'MyClass<int>'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the role of 'typename' in a class template?

Options.OptionText: To create an alias for a type

m_Item1.Explanation: 'typename' is used to declare a type parameter in templates.

m_Item1.QuestionText: When is the code inside a class template instantiated?
m_Item1.Explanation: Class template code is instantiated at compile time when a template is used.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Can a class template have multiple type parameters?

Options.OptionText: Only if they are of the same type

m_Item1.Explanation: A class template can have multiple type parameters separated by commas.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How would you define a class template that holds a pair of values of potentially different types?
m_Item1.Explanation: The correct declaration for holding a pair of values is 'template<class T1, class T2>'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the main advantage of using class templates?
m_Item1.Explanation: Class templates help eliminate code duplication by allowing generic definitions.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is not a usage of class templates?

Options.OptionText: Creating a vector to store any type
Developing a stack for any type

m_Item1.Explanation: Class templates are not directly used for overloading operators; they define generic types.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What happens when a class template's type parameter is not used in the class?

Options.OptionText: It results in a syntax error

m_Item1.Explanation: If a type parameter is not used, it generally works without errors but is not efficient.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you define a default type for a class template parameter?
m_Item1.Explanation: You can define default template parameters with 'template<class T = int>'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which statement is true about class templates and inheritance?

Options.OptionText: A template class cannot be derived
A template class can inherit only from another template class
A non-template class can inherit from a template class
Inheritance is not possible with template classes

m_Item1.Explanation: A non-template class can derive from a template class by specifying a type.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the correct way to specialize a template for a specific type?
m_Item1.Explanation: To specialize a template for a type, you use 'template<> class MyClass<int>'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following cannot be used as a template type parameter?
m_Item1.Explanation: Void cannot be used as a template type parameter since it doesn't represent a type that can be instantiated.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What error occurs if a template class is instantiated with an invalid type?
m_Item1.Explanation: A compiler error occurs if a template is instantiated with an invalid type.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Can a member function of a class template be defined outside the class template?

Options.OptionText: Only if it's declared in a public section

m_Item1.Explanation: Member functions of class templates can be defined outside the class using the qualified name.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does 'template<class T> class A {}' represent?
m_Item1.Explanation: This represents the definition of a class template with one type parameter.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which access specifier is not compatible with class template parameters?
m_Item1.Explanation: All standard access specifiers are compatible with class templates.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Is it possible to create non-type parameters in class templates?

Options.OptionText: No, they must all be types

m_Item1.Explanation: Class templates can have non-type parameters, but they must be integral constants, according to C++ standards.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A common activity performed on a container is called
m_Item1.Explanation: every container has common activity that is traversal.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Vectors have advantage of direct access that is also called as
m_Item1.Explanation: vector is dynamic growing array , so it has facility to access any element using subscript operator([]) and that's why it has random access.

m_Item1.QuestionText: An iterator is an object that has ability to traverse
m_Item1.Explanation: Iterator is used to travel over elements from container,so depend upon type of that  container is holding it  travels

m_Item1.QuestionText: Pick out the correct statement about string template?

Options.OptionText: It is used to replace a string.
It is used to replace a string with another string at runtime.
It is used to delete a string.

m_Item1.Explanation: Every string template is used to replace the string with another string at runtime.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
What is the output of this program?
#include <iostream>
#include <string>
using namespace std;
template < typename T >
void print_mydata (T output)
{
  cout << output << endl;
}
int main ()
{
  double d = 5.5;
  string s ("Hello World");
  print_mydata (d);
  print_mydata (s);
  return 0;
}
m_Item1.Explanation: In this program, We are passing the value to the template and printing it in the template. Initially we are passing double value and then string value.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is a function template

Options.OptionText: creating a function without specify the exact type
creating a function with an exact type
creating a function without  blank spaces

m_Item1.Explanation: Function template is used to create a function without having to specify the exact type. .

m_Item1.QuestionText:
#include <iostream>  
using namespace std;
template < typename type > class TestVirt
{
public:
  virtual type TestFunct (type Var1)
  {
    return Var1 * 2;
  }
};

int main ()
{
  TestVirt < int >Var1;
  cout << Var1.TestFunct (100) << endl;
  return 0;
}
m_Item1.Explanation:
In this program, We are using class to pass the value and then we are manipulating it.
Here we have template class TestVirt which has TestFunct method which take value and return value*2 hence when we pass 100 to testFunct it returns 200.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the validity of template parameters?
m_Item1.Explanation: Template parameters are valid inside a block only i.e. they have block scope.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is iterator class?

Options.OptionText: A class that is used to traverse through the objects maintained by a container class
A class that is used to traverse through the other class

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the STL containers store the elements contiguously(in adjacent memory locations)?
m_Item1.Explanation: A vector is a sequence container class that implements dynamic array, means size automatically changes when appending elements. A vector stores the elements in contiguous memory locations and allocates the memory as needed at run time.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
What is the output of this program?
#include <iostream>
#include <string>
using namespace std;
int main ()
{
string str ("microsoft");
string::reverse_iterator r;
for (r = str.rbegin() ; r < str.rend(); r++ )
cout << *r;
return 0;
}
m_Item1.Explanation: Reverse iterators iterate backwards: increasing them moves them towards the beginning of the container.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Each generic type in a template function definition is preceded by the keyword_____.
m_Item1.Explanation: template<class type> void Swap(type,type) is syntax of template function.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
What is the output?
#include<iostream>
#include<vector>
using namespace std; 
void main()
{
       vector<int>v(5);
       vector<int>::iterator i;
       int n = 1;
       for (n=1, i = v.begin();i !=v.end();i++,n++)
              *i= n;
 
       v.push_back(23);
       cout << " Size: "<< v.size();
       cout << " Capacity:: " << v.capacity();
}
m_Item1.Explanation:
The vector size is the total number of elements of a vector and it always the same for all compilers. To get the size of a vector, vector::size() function is used.
Capacity is the memory space occupied by a vector; elements of a vector are stored as an array in the memory. Thus, the capacity is the amount of space which is currently using by the vector (or internal array)

m_Item1.QuestionText: In the template <class T> declaration, T stands for
m_Item1.Explanation: A class template provides a specification for generating classes based on parameters. Class templates are generally used to implement containers. Where in declaration T is generic data type.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which one supports unknown data types in a single framework?
m_Item1.Explanation: Templates are powerful features of C++ which allows you to write generic programs. In simple terms, you can create a single function or a class to work with different data types using templates. Templates are often used in larger codebase for the purpose of code reusability and flexibility of the programs.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
#include <iostream>
using namespace std;
template<typename type>
type Max(type Var1, type Var2)
{
      return Var1 > Var2 ? Var1:Var2;
}
int main()
{
       int p;
        p = Max(100, 200);
        cout << p << endl;
        return 0;
}
m_Item1.Explanation: In above code Max is template fucntion which isl work for all data type.And it is returning max between two elements.
